name: Amr ibn Dabi'a
title: Awakened Repentant
shortDescription: A man from the Taym tribe who initially came to Karbala with Umar Sa'd's army, but repented and joined the Imam.
fullStory:
Amr ibn Dabi'a al-Taymi was a brave, veteran man and a renowned horseman of Kufa. Like Hur ibn Yazid al-Riyahi and some others, he initially came to Karbala with Umar Sa'd's army intending to fight Imam Hussain (AS).

In the days before Ashura, when Amr observed that Umar Sa'd and Ibn Ziyad were rejecting all of Imam Hussain's (AS) peaceful proposals and insisting on war and bloodshed, his awakened conscience brought him to his senses.

Amr realized that the Kufan army was on the side of falsehood and the Prophet's (PBUH) son was on the side of truth. Therefore, he secretly defected from Umar Sa'd's camp, joined Imam Hussain's (AS) luminous camp, and repented.

On the day of Ashura, Amr ibn Dabi'a, who was now a defender of Wilayah, bravely drew his sword with the start of Yazid's army's all-out attack. He fought manfully and, after repelling many attacks, was martyred in that very first attack, achieving a blessed end through his repentance.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عَمْرِو بْنِ ضُبَيْعَةَ الضَّبَعِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Amr ibn Dabi'a.
